result: If I could change the world in one way, I would make sure that every person—regardless of where they’re born—has reliable access to clean water, nutritious food, basic health care, and a quality education. Those foundations are the springboard for everything else: health, opportunity, innovation, and the empathy that lets societies thrive. When basic needs are met for everyone, the ripple effects would lift countless other issues—from poverty and inequality to environmental stewardship and peace. In short, guaranteeing universal access to the essentials of life would give humanity the platform it needs to build a more just, sustainable, and flourishing world.

When basic needs are met for everyone, the ripple effects would lift countless other issues—from poverty and inequality to environmental stewardship and peace. In short, guaranteeing universal access to the essentials of life would give humanity the platform it needs to build a more just, sustainable, and flourishing world.
